title: Ohio Expensing Conformity Will Boost Innovation and Competitiveness
summary: Ohio's SB 9 will boost economic growth by conforming Ohio's tax code to the domestic research and experimentation (R&E) immediate cost recovery provision in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act.

title: Pharmaceutical Tariffs Would Add Costs and Reduce Innovation
summary: Thought the Trump administration has not yet imposed new, industry-specific tariffs on drugs or active pharmaceutical ingredients, such tariffs would be a hidden cost on Americans: they would shrink incomes, reduce investment, and lead to less innovation.

title: FAQs About Border Adjustment
summary: What is a border adjustment? Is it a new idea? How would it work in practice?

title: Online Sports Betting Could Be Lucrative for Alaska if New Legislation Gets the Taxes Right
summary: Alaskan lawmakers are actively debating this session whether they should open their digital borders to the online sports betting market. If legalization is a step Alaska decides to take, the right tax mix could result in notable fiscal advantages for the state.

title: Utah’s Digital Ad Tax, by Any Other Name, Is Just as Legally Fraught
summary: A digital advertising tax in Utah will increase costs for Utah businesses and expose the state to complicated legal battles.

title: Tracking Three IRS Datapoints to Watch During the 2026 Tax Filing Season
summary: The total amount refunded as of the first week of reporting is running ahead of past filing season at $19.95 billion, while the average refund is up 10.9 percent at $2,290.

title: Tariffs Increased Retail Prices of Imports by 7 Percentage Points Prior to Supreme Court Ruling
summary: President Trump and those in his administration have insisted that consumers are not bearing any of the tariffs. But the latest data show exactly the opposite.

title: Trump Tariffs Threaten to Offset Much of the “Big Beautiful Bill” Tax Cuts
summary: The tariffs now in effect threaten to offset much of the GDP growth from the tax cuts, while falling short of paying for them.

title: 2025 European Tax Policy Scorecard
summary: The variety of approaches to taxation among European countries creates a need to evaluate these systems relative to each other. For that purpose, we have developed the European Tax Policy Scorecard—a relative comparison of European countries’ tax systems.

title: Vaping Taxes by State, 2026
summary: The vaping industry has grown rapidly in recent decades, becoming a well-established product category and a viable alternative to cigarettes for those trying to quit smoking. US states levy a variety of tax structures on vaping products.

title: Trump Tariffs Blocked by the Supreme Court (What’s Next?)
summary: Last Friday, the Supreme Court struck down President Trump’s IEEPA tariffs in a landmark 6-3 decision.

title: US Taxpayers to See a Nearly $2,300 Average Tax Cut in 2026 Under the Big Beautiful Bill
summary: Notably, the OBBBA makes permanent the individual tax changes first put in place by the TCJA, which avoids a tax hike on an estimated 62 percent of tax filers in 2026.

title: Tax Treatment of the Pass-Through Business Sector: A Primer
summary: The pass-through business sector—including partnerships, S corporations, and sole proprietorships—employs most of the private-sector workforce in the US and accounts for about half of US business income.

title: Diesel and Gas Taxes in Europe, 2026
summary: Fuel taxes continue to be a central policy consideration for European countries amid geopolitical conflicts, increased emphasis on environmental policy, and economic conditions experienced by average consumers.

title: 2026 State of the Union: Five Things to Know
summary: President Trump is scheduled to deliver the annual State of the Union address to Congress. The speech is bound to address a multitude of topics, but taxes and (especially) tariffs will certainly feature prominently.

title: Tariff Tracker: Impact of Trump Tariffs & Trade War by the Numbers
summary: The Trump tariffs have not meaningfully altered the trade balance and amount to an average tax increase per US household of $700 in 2026.

title: Supreme Court Strikes Down President Trump’s Tariffs
summary: The Supreme Court ruling against the IEEPA tariffs provides immediate relief to US businesses and workers and is a welcome rebuke of President Trump’s overreach of executive authority to unilaterally impose significant tax hikes on the US economy.

title: Poland Considering a Second Harmful Digital Tax
summary: Poland is proposing to broaden and raise its digital services tax (DST) from 1.5 percent to 3 percent. Because DSTs tax revenues, not profits, a company with a 10 percent profit margin would face a 30 percent effective tax rate on digital services provided in Poland.

title: Expect a Busy Year for Alternative Tobacco Product Taxes
summary: Coming into 2026, several states are eyeing new or increased Alternative Tobacco Product (ATP) taxes.

title: Taxing Alternative Nicotine in New York Is a Health Policy Buzzkill
summary: As states grapple with taxing the evolving tobacco and nicotine market, policymakers should carefully consider the tax mix they implement.
